treatments.Apple___Apple_scab: Apply fungicides such as captan or sulfur. Remove and destroy fallen leaves to prevent overwintering.
treatments.Apple___Black_rot: Prune out infected branches and remove mummified fruit. Apply fungicides like captan during the growing season.
treatments.Apple___Cedar_apple_rust: Remove nearby juniper trees if possible. Use fungicides like myclobutanil early in the spring.
treatments.Apple___healthy: No treatment needed. Maintain regular pruning and fertilization.
treatments.Blueberry___healthy: No treatment needed. Ensure acidic soil (pH 4.5-5.5).
treatments.Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew: Prune to improve air circulation. Use sulfur-based fungicides or potassium bicarbonate.
treatments.Cherry_(including_sour)___healthy: No treatment needed. Monitor for pests regularly.
treatments.Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray: Rotate crops and manage residue. Apply triazole or strobilurin fungicides if infection is severe.
treatments.Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_: Use resistant hybrids. Fungicides are usually only necessary for high-value seed corn.
treatments.Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight: Practice crop rotation and use resistant hybrids. Tillage can help reduce inoculum.
treatments.Corn_(maize)___healthy: No treatment needed. Ensure proper nitrogen levels.
treatments.Grape___Black_rot: Prune for airflow and remove old fruit clusters. Apply fungicides like mancozeb or captan.
treatments.Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles): Protect pruning wounds with fungicide paste. No direct chemical cure; focus on vine health.
treatments.Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo: Improve ventilation through pruning. Use copper-based fungicides if symptoms persist.
treatments.Grape___healthy: No treatment needed. Maintain trellis system.
treatments.Orange___Haunglongbing_(Citrus_greening): Remove infected trees immediately to prevent spread. Control psyllid insect vectors with insecticides.
treatments.Peach___Bacterial_spot: Avoid overhead irrigation. Apply copper-based sprays during dormancy and early season.
treatments.Peach___healthy: No treatment needed. Thin fruit to prevent branch breakage.
treatments.Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot: Use pathogen-free seeds. Apply copper fungicides. Avoid working in wet fields.
treatments.Pepper,_bell___healthy: No treatment needed. Maintain consistent moisture.
treatments.Potato___Early_blight: Maintain plant vigor with proper fertilizer. Apply fungicides containing chlorothalonil or mancozeb.
treatments.Potato___Late_blight: Immediately remove and destroy infected plants. Use fungicides like metalaxyl or copper-based sprays.
treatments.Potato___healthy: No treatment needed. Practice 3-year crop rotation.
treatments.Raspberry___healthy: No treatment needed. Prune old canes after harvest.
treatments.Soybean___healthy: No treatment needed. Monitor for soybean aphids.
treatments.Squash___Powdery_mildew: Plant in full sun with good spacing. Use neem oil or sulfur-based fungicides.
treatments.Strawberry___Leaf_scorch: Plant resistant varieties. Remove old leaves. Avoid excessive nitrogen in spring.
treatments.Strawberry___healthy: No treatment needed. Mulch with straw to keep berries off the soil.
treatments.Tomato___Bacterial_spot: Use certified seeds. Apply copper fungicides and avoid overhead watering.
treatments.Tomato___Early_blight: Remove lower leaves to prevent soil splash. Use fungicides like maneb or chlorothalonil.
treatments.Tomato___Late_blight: Highly contagious. Remove infected plants. Apply copper fungicides or chlorothalonil regularly during wet weather.
treatments.Tomato___Leaf_Mold: Increase greenhouse ventilation and reduce humidity. Use resistant varieties.
treatments.Tomato___Septoria_leaf_spot: Avoid overhead watering. Use mulch to prevent soil spores from splashing onto leaves.
treatments.Tomato___Spider_mites Two-spotted_spider: Use insecticidal soap or neem oil. Increase humidity around the plants.
treatments.Tomato___Target_Spot: Improve airflow and keep foliage dry. Apply fungicides like azoxystrobin.
treatments.Tomato___Tomato_Yellow_Leaf_Curl_Virus: Control whitefly populations with yellow sticky traps or insecticides. Use resistant varieties.
treatments.Tomato___Tomato_mosaic_virus: Remove and burn infected plants. Clean tools with bleach. No known chemical cure.
treatments.Tomato___healthy: No treatment needed. Use cages or stakes for support.
